input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual (detail) routes must be registered explicitly:

```csharp
// In AppShell constructor or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

Then navigate with:
```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails");
```

Only routes declared as `ShellContent`/`Tab`/`FlyoutItem` in XAML are auto-registered.

---

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` navigates but nothing visible changes**

The route resolves, but the visual tree doesn't update — most likely because your `ShellContent` uses `ContentTemplate` (deferred rendering) **and** the `Content` property is also set, or you have duplicate route segments. Fix: ensure you're using `DataTemplate`:

```xml
<ShellContent Route="active"
              Title="Active"
              ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActiveProductsPage}" />
```

Also verify the **full route hierarchy** matches. Use `Shell.Current.CurrentState.Location` to debug. A common pitfall: a parent `Tab` or `FlyoutItem` lacks a `Route`, so `//products/active` doesn't resolve to what you expect. Explicitly set `Route` on every ancestor:

```xml
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <Tab Route="main">
        <ShellContent Route="active" ... />
    </Tab>
</FlyoutItem>
```

---

**3. 3-second startup with 12 pages**

Shell creates pages eagerly when you use `Content` or `ShellContent.Content`. Switch every `ShellContent` to **`ContentTemplate`** so pages are created lazily (only when first navigated to):

```xml
<!-- ❌ Eager — page created at startup -->
<ShellContent>
    <local:MyPage />
</ShellContent>

<!-- ✅ Lazy — page created on first visit -->
<ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:MyPage}" />
```

With 12 pages, this alone typically drops startup from seconds to sub-second. If still slow, also check for heavy work in page constructors — move it to `OnAppearing` or use `async` initialization.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known problems with known solutions. Let me provide concise, expert answers.
